concept: Inventory / Time-Bounded Record of Held Items and Their Status
description: A record of what is held: items, quantities, places, conditions, within a scope, at a time. An inventory represents the stock, 'muphera' (stock), without being it; the shelf keeps its own truth, and the record's accuracy ends the moment conditions change without it.
sound_symbolism: Hissing 'si' draws a fine bright opening, and stressed 'ta' answers at the teeth with a broad firm beat. Final 'wi' rounds the lips and glides back toward bright 'i'. Wide at its center, the word folds neatly inward at the end.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'mia ha sitawi tukiro.' (I review the inventory.) State the scope, time, method, recorder, units, and uncertainty where relevant. Distinct from 'muphera' (stock), 'sirami' (record), 'tawi' (count), 'phelure' (store), and ownership documentation.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Shared inventories make material capacity visible for repair, reuse, mutual aid, and ecological planning while keeping records distinct from physical reality.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A time-bounded inventory invites correction and prevents an old or incomplete record from being treated as unquestionable proof of what exists or who controls it.

tags.cognition: a structured representation of material state
tags.temporal: a record explicitly bounded to a time
